name: FFN / MLP
oneliner: The position-wise fully connected sub-block in each transformer layer — up-project, activate, down-project. Stores ~67% of each layer's parameters.
explanation: Operates independently per token (no cross-token interaction). Serves as the 'memory/knowledge storage' component — factual knowledge and patterns predominantly stored in FFN weights. Standard design: project up to larger intermediate dim, apply activation, project back down. In original Transformer, d_ff = 4 $\times$ d_model with ReLU. Modern models use SwiGLU with 3 matrices and d_ff $\approx$ 2.67 $\times$ d_model to keep total params constant. FFN is the dominant parameter consumer: ~67% of layer params in gated variants.
fundamentals: Standard: FFN(x) = $W_2$·activation($W_1$·x). Gated (SwiGLU): FFN(x) = (Swish(xW_gate) ⊙ xW_up)W_down. LLaMA 7B: d_model=4096, d_ff=11008. FFN params: 3$\times$4096$\times$11008 = 135M vs Attention: 4$\times$409$6^2$ = 67M. The '2/3 of 4x' rule: d_ff $\approx$ (8/3)$\times$d_model, rounded to multiple of 256 for hardware efficiency.
sources: Vaswani et al., 'Attention Is All You Need,' NeurIPS 2017, arXiv:1706.03762

resources.label: Attention Is All You Need (original Transformer)
